options.a: a physical condition caused by a lack of oxygen to meet the needs of the body tissues, causing impaired thinking, poor judgement, and slow reactions.
options.b: often produced during steep turns when pilots turn their heads in a direction opposite to the direction in which the aircraft is turning.
options.c: a condition of lacking oxygen in the brain causing the circulatory system to compensate by decreasing the heart rate.
options.d: a physical condition caused by a lack of oxygen saturation in the blood while hyperventilating.
